title: Rytidostylis trilobata
intro: Rytidostylis trilobata, also known as the Devil's Claw or Grapple Plant, is a flowering plant native to the Kalahari Desert in southern Africa. The roots and rhizomes of Rytidostylis trilobata have been used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health issues, including arthritis, pain, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include harpagoside, a iridoid glycoside, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Rytidostylis trilobata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and teas, often in combination with other anti-inflammatory herbs to provide relief from joint pain and inflammation.
